## Ethereum Price Trend & Market Sentiment — June 2026

### Research Limitations
All web-based data retrieval tools (WebSearch, WebFetch, ToolSearch) were unavailable during this research session. ### What Is Known (Pre-existing Knowledge, Cutoff Early 2025)
- Ethereum transitioned to Proof-of-Stake via "The Merge" in September 2022, fundamentally changing its tokenomics (reduced issuance, potential deflationary pressure via EIP-1559 fee burning).
- Major Ethereum upgrades through 2024-2025 included Dencun (proto-danksharding, EIP-4844) which reduced Layer 2 transaction costs significantly.
- Spot Ethereum ETFs were approved and launched in the US in mid-2024, opening institutional access.
- Ethereum's price historically correlates with broader crypto market cycles, Bitcoin halving cycles, and macroeconomic conditions (interest rates, risk appetite).

key_facts: All web data retrieval tools (WebSearch, WebFetch, ToolSearch) were unavailable, preventing live data collection on ETH price and sentiment for June 2026.
Ethereum completed its Proof-of-Stake transition (The Merge) in September 2022, reducing energy consumption by ~99.95% and changing token issuance dynamics.
EIP-1559 (August 2021) introduced a base fee burning mechanism, making ETH potentially deflationary during periods of high network activity.
The Dencun upgrade (March 2024) introduced proto-danksharding (EIP-4844), significantly reducing Layer 2 transaction costs via blob transactions.
Spot Ethereum ETFs were approved and launched in the US in mid-2024, providing regulated institutional investment vehicles.
Bitcoin's most recent halving occurred in April 2024; historically, crypto bull market peaks have followed 12-18 months after Bitcoin halvings.
Ethereum's price is influenced by macroeconomic factors including Federal Reserve interest rate policy, risk appetite, and dollar strength.
